channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: El psicólogo ganador del Nobel que creyó encontrar el secreto de la felicidad
item.description: La necesidad de seguir buscando algo mejor ha envenenado hasta los momentos más mundanos.
item.dc:creator: David Epstein

item.title: ‘He Didn’t Sound Like Someone to Be Messed With’
item.description: Extra effort to hail a cab, an apology for a subway encounter and more reader tales of New York City in this week’s Metropolitan Diary.

item.title: After a Rough Start, Mamdani Focuses on Skeptical Black New Yorkers
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has more explicitly attempted to address specific concerns of Black New Yorkers with his policy agenda, but some remain wary.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani said he had heard from Black leaders and residents about how policy decisions had excluded them.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 17, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: The Value in Recording Hard Conversations
item.description: How a tape can preserve a connection to reality — and each other.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Jackson

media:content.media:credit: Cornell Watson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Nick Woomer-Deters, 46, from North Carolina. He was on “The Daily” episode.

item.title: A North Korean Soccer Team Makes a Rare Visit to South Korea
item.description: The team’s trip, for a women’s soccer tournament, is unlikely to catalyze a diplomatic thaw between the two Koreas, although some sports events have.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Members of Naegohyang, a North Korean women’s soccer team, arriving at Incheon International Airport in South Korea on Sunday.

item.title: Así vivieron el brote de hantavirus los pasajeros del crucero MV Hondius
item.description: El brote de hantavirus en el MV Hondius hizo saltar las alarmas en un mundo aún traumatizado por la covid. Para los que iban a bordo, el peligro estaba mucho más cerca.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz, Claire Moses, Amelia Nierenberg and Samuel Granados

item.title: W.H.O. Declares Ebola Outbreak a Global Health Emergency
item.description: Just a day after the outbreak was announced, cases were confirmed in the capitals of Uganda and the Democratic Republic of Congo. There is no approved vaccine for the strain.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:description: A man is carried from an ambulance as he arrives Monday at a hospital after confirmation of an Ebola outbreak in the Ituri Province of the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: The First Roundup of Jews in Paris, 1941
item.description: A new collection of photographs gives us a new picture of the Holocaust in France.
item.dc:creator: Jean-Marc Dreyfus

item.title: My Classmate, ChatGPT
item.description: Lessons from the first graduating A.I. class.
item.dc:creator: Theo Baker

item.title: Corrections: May 17, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Sunday, May 17, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: For Migrants Deported to Congo, an ‘Unspoken Prison’
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, May 17, 2026.

item.title: 7 Republicans Voted to Convict Trump. Most Are No Longer in Office.
item.description: Senator Bill Cassidy’s defeat means no more than two of them will be left in Congress next year.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Bill Cassidy, a Republican from Louisiana, voted to convict Mr. Trump in his second impeachment trial.

item.title: Senate Ruling Threatens Ballroom Funding in G.O.P. Budget Bill
item.description: Democrats announced Saturday night that the Senate’s top parliamentary referee had determined that the $1 billion provision did not comply with budget rules.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.category: White House East Wing and Ballroom (Washington, DC)

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Crews continued work on the White House ballroom on Friday.

item.title: Cassidy Loses Senate Primary in Louisiana, as Trump Vanquishes G.O.P. Foe
item.description: Senator Bill Cassidy, a two-term Republican who voted to convict President Trump in his 2021 impeachment trial, could not muster enough votes to continue to a runoff next month.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold

media:content.media:credit: Emily Kask for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Bill Cassidy speaking at a watch party in Baton Rouge on Saturday after losing his primary contest.

item.title: Venezuela Extradites Billionaire Tycoon Alex Saab to U.S.
item.description: The extradition of Alex Saab, who is tied to a huge graft scheme, is part of a purge of powerful figures who helped the deposed president, Nicolás Maduro, stay in power.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero

media:content.media:description: Alex Saab, center, in Caracas, Venezuela, in 2023, was once among key allies of the deposed Venezuelan president, Nicolás Maduro.

item.title: Bulgaria Wins Eurovision Song Contest as Israel Comes Second
item.description: Five countries boycotted this year’s contest over Israel’s involvement, but politics took a back seat once the show in Vienna got underway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall

media:content.media:description: Dara, center, won the competition after topping both the jury and audience votes by large margins.

item.title: Shoppers’ Frenzy for ‘Royal Pop’ Pocket Watches Forces Swatch to Shut Stores
item.description: The pocket watches, a collaboration with the luxury timepiece maker Audemars Piguet, drew crowds to stores and malls around the world.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:description: Customers had been waiting outside a Swatch store in Geneva. The company closed dozens of its stores on Saturday as crowds lined up to snag its new pocket watch, a collaboration with Audemars Piguet.

item.title: The Fight for Voting Rights Returns to Selma
item.description: A crowd gathered at a pivotal site of defiance in the civil rights movement, this time in opposition to efforts by Republicans to eliminate majority-Black districts.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas

media:content.media:description: “We gather on this day in mourning, in lamentation, in outrage, and in deep, profound love,” the Rev. Sofía Betancourt said from the pulpit of Tabernacle Baptist Church in Selma.

item.title: China Indicates Tariffs Were Discussed at Trump Summit
item.description: China’s Ministry of Commerce said Saturday that the countries had struck a preliminary agreement to reduce some tariffs, seemingly contradicting statements by President Trump
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson

media:content.media:description: President Trump said Friday that he had not discussed U.S. tariffs on imports from China, a key issue going into the summit with China’s leader, Xi Jinping.

item.title: Thousands of FiveThirtyEight Articles Seemingly Vanish From the Internet
item.description: The influential polling analysis site was shut down last year, but an earlier archived version, fivethirtyeight.com, had lived on. Now the site is redirecting users to ABC News.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

media:content.media:description: Nate Silver, center, in 2015. Mr. Silver founded FiveThirtyEight as a blog in 2008 before building it into a highly influential polling analysis operation.

item.title: Double Meanings
item.description: Derrick Niederman drops a heavy-duty Sunday crossword on us.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: Don’t expect to fly through this prickly puzzle.

item.title: Train Crashes Into Bangkok Traffic, Killing at Least 8 People
item.description: A freight train crashed into traffic on one of Bangkok’s busiest roads on Saturday. At least eight people were killed and dozens were injured, Thai officials said.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: Emergency services at the site of the collision in Bangkok on Saturday.

item.title: Car Rams into Pedestrians in Northern Italy, Injuring at Least 8
item.description: A driver rammed a car into a group of people on the main shopping street of Modena, in northern Italy. Four of the injured were in serious condition, the city’s mayor said.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:description: The police patrolling the scene after a car crashed into pedestrians in Modena, Italy, on Saturday.

item.title: Long Island Rail Road Service Suspended as Workers Strike
item.description: For the first time in over 30 years, thousands of workers walked off the job, halting service on the Long Island Rail Road, the country’s busiest passenger rail service.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The last Long Island Rail Road strike was in 1994, when a two-day suspension shut down the service.

item.title: Félicien Kabuga Dies; Accused of Masterminding Rwanda’s Genocide
item.description: One of his country’s richest businessmen, he fled arrest and escaped prosecution for allegedly financing and directing the bloodletting that cost 800,000 lives in 1994.
item.dc:creator: Alan Cowell
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:description: A screen grab of Félicien Kabuga at a hearing in The Hague, where he faced charges of genocide and crimes against humanity, in 2022.

item.title: Finland, the Eurovision Favorite, Is Here for Revenge
item.description: The pop star Pete Parkkonen and the violinist Linda Lampenius performed “Liekinheitin” on a stage covered in flames.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall

media:content.media:description: Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen setting Eurovision on fire in Tuesday’s semifinal.

item.title: Woman Gives Birth in Brooklyn Courtroom
item.description: A woman who was nine months pregnant was in court on low-level charges, raising questions about why she was discharged from a hospital before the hearing.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Southall

media:content.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Organizations including the Legal Aid Society called for an investigation into the circumstances surrounding a woman going into labor in a courtroom.

item.title: Israel’s Noam Bettan Brings French Flair to Eurovision Amid Controversy
item.description: The singer performed a pop track largely in French, as the show faced boycotts from other countries over Israel’s military actions in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall

media:content.media:description: Noam Bettan of Israel performing “Michelle” in Tuesday night’s semifinal.

item.title: Kansas Judge Blocks Law Banning Gender-Transition Treatments for Youths
item.description: Two transgender teenagers claimed that the ban, enacted last year over the Democratic governor’s veto, violated the Kansas Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith and Edgar Sandoval

media:content.media:credit: Evert Nelson/Topeka Capital-Journal, via USA Today, via Imagn
media:content.media:description: Judge Carl Folsom III temporarily blocked significant portions of a Kansas law.

item.title: Severe Storms to Bring Strong Winds and Hail to Central United States
item.description: A series of storms, including supercells, threatens to dump rain, hail and create a tornado risk for a vast section of the country. The most severe weather is expected on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

item.title: Mamdani’s Nakba Day Social Media Post Marks Shift for N.Y.C. Mayors
item.description: Pro-Palestinian New Yorkers celebrated Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s decision to post about the Palestinians’ day of remembrance, but the move was criticized by some Jewish leaders.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack, Dana Rubinstein and Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani on Friday posted on social media a four-minute, documentary-style video that featured an interview with a woman named Inea Bushnaq.

item.title: Who Is JJ? The 2025 Eurovision Winner Kicks Off This Year’s Show
item.description: JJ, from Austria, kicked off this year’s Eurovision grand final in Vienna, as the contest celebrates its 70th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli

media:content.media:description: JJ of Austria, the winner of last year’s Eurovision, performing during this year’s Eurovision Song Contest in Vienna on Thursday.

item.title: Car Plows Into Pedestrians in Northern Italian Town, Injuring at Least 8
item.description: Four of the injured were in serious condition, the mayor of Modena said, praising residents who rushed to stop the car’s driver from escaping on foot.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo

media:content.media:description: The police patrolling the scene after a car crashed into pedestrians in Modena, Italy, on Saturday.

item.title: Voters in Louisiana Head to the Polls, Uncertain but Determined
item.description: Votes cast for the House candidates on Saturday’s primary ballot won’t count after state officials moved the election to November to provide time to redraw congressional maps.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane

media:content.media:credit: Annie Flanagan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voters in New Orleans and across Louisiana were also considering candidates for the Senate primary election and a series of constitutional amendments.

item.title: Louisiana Primary 2026: Live Election Results
item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Louisiana primary elections.

item.title: At Supreme Court, Virginia Democrats Pressed Legal Theory Embraced by Trump
item.description: A long-shot emergency request to restore a voting map was the latest salvo in an all-out legal war over control of the House.
item.dc:creator: Adam Liptak

media:content.media:description: The justices on Friday rejected the application without comment or noted dissent.

item.title: Louisiana U.S. Senate Primary 2026: Live Election Results
item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Louisiana primary election.

item.title: Eurovision 2026: Bulgaria Wins. Israel Takes Second.
item.description: Dara and her catchy dance track, “Bangaranga,” secured Bulgaria the victory with a big surge from the audience vote.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
